common.alreadySetReplace: Already set — enter a new value to replace it

bank.removeConfirm:
Remove the bank “{name}”?

Only the triage data (decisions, scores, thumbnails) is deleted — the source folder and its images are NOT touched.

page.title: Image bank
page.description: Point the app at a big unsorted folder (a Telegram export, a scrape dump…) and triage it into dataset-ready selections. Quality checks flag blur, noise, flat or small shots and group near-duplicates; face analysis sorts by person. Promote the keepers into a dataset when ready. The source folder is never modified.
page.name: Name
page.loadFailed: Could not load the image banks.
page.createFailed: Could not create the image bank.
page.removed: Image bank removed — source folder untouched.
page.removeFailed: Could not remove the image bank.
page.empty: No image bank yet — create one above to start triaging a folder.

workspace.autoRejectResult: Auto-reject: {count} image(s) rejected ({flags}). Manual ✓/✕ choices were not changed.
workspace.selectedAll: {count} image(s) selected across the whole filter and all pages.
workspace.scanTitle: Score every unscanned image for sharpness, noise, flatness and size; hash it and group near-duplicates. CPU only, runs in the background.
workspace.rescanTitle: Re-score everything, for example after files changed on disk.
workspace.peopleTitle: Detect the dominant face in every non-rejected image and cluster the bank by person. No reference is needed. Runs on CPU and may take a while for thousands of images.
workspace.peopleSetup: Install the Quality tools in Setup to sort by person.
workspace.scoreTitle: Rate every non-rejected image for aesthetics (1–10), flag NSFW and group by visual style in one CLIP pass. Enables a smarter “keep best”. Uses the GPU when available and runs in the background.
workspace.scoreSetup: Install the Image bank scoring extra under Setup → Quality tools to score aesthetics, NSFW and style.
workspace.watermarkTitle: Scan every non-rejected image for an overlaid watermark, logo or URL with the Qwen3-VL detector. Detection only: the image bank never edits source files.
workspace.watermarkSetup: Pull the vision model under Settings → Captioning & quality to scan for watermarks.
workspace.autoRejectTitle: Bulk-reject undecided images carrying the selected quality flags.
workspace.autoRejectHelp: Rejects only UNDECIDED images with the selected flags. Manual ✓/✕ choices are never changed, everything remains reversible, and no source file is deleted.
workspace.promoteTitle: Copy the kept selection into a dataset.
workspace.duplicatesTitle: Near-duplicate groups with their resolution panel.

duplicates.loadFailed: Could not load the duplicate groups.
duplicates.empty: No unresolved duplicate groups. The image bank is clean or every group has already been resolved. Groups appear after a quality scan.
duplicates.reversible: Losing images are rejected, never deleted. Undo a decision from the ✕ Rejected filter.
duplicates.resolveAllBestTitle: In every group, keep the highest-resolution and then sharpest member; reject the rest.
duplicates.resolveAllFirstTitle: In every group, keep the first member in import order; reject the rest.
duplicates.clickToKeep: {count} image(s) — click the one to KEEP

promote.title: Promote to dataset
promote.started: Promotion started — follow the progress bar.
promote.selectionDescription: The {count} selected image(s) will be COPIED into the dataset, normalized to WebP, with existing near-duplicates skipped. The image bank and source folder stay unchanged.
promote.keptDescription: The {count} kept image(s) not yet promoted will be COPIED into the dataset, normalized to WebP, with existing near-duplicates skipped. The image bank and source folder stay unchanged.
promote.noDatasets: No dataset yet — create one on the Datasets page first, then promote.

folderPicker.description: Folders on the machine running the app. Nothing is opened or modified; you are only selecting a location.

helpMode.on: Help mode is on — click any ? badge to jump to the guide
helpMode.off: Turn on Help mode to reveal ? badges that link to the guide

updates.restarting: ↻ Updated — the app is restarting. This page reloads automatically when it’s back…

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.importBackupTitle: Import a portable dataset backup — a new dataset will be created

kindHint.character: A person/face — identity binds to the trigger
kindHint.concept: A recurring act/effect — the concept binds to the trigger
kindHint.style: An always-on aesthetic: load the LoRA and control its influence with the LoRA weight

pipeline.intro: Build a consistent character: one reference photo becomes a curated, captioned training set for a LoRA you can use in every generator.
pipeline.referenceText: Upload one clear photo of the face.
pipeline.curateText: Synthesize varied shots, keep the best ones.
pipeline.trainText: Export or train — reuse the character anywhere.

empty.title: No datasets yet
empty.bodyAfter: — one reference photo is enough to start generating a full training set.

form.commonTrigger: “{trigger}” looks like a common word — the base model already has a meaning for it. Prefer a unique token like {suggestion}.
form.targetModelHint: — sets the caption style & groups the menu (changeable later)
form.fidelityHint: — what the LoRA must reproduce (changeable later)
form.faceHint: Identity = the face. Body shape may vary with the prompt.
form.bodyHint: Total fidelity: body shape, tattoos and marks bind to the trigger too. Prefers full-frame imports and more bust/body shots.
form.conceptRequired: (required — it will be omitted from every caption)
form.conceptPlaceholder: Describe the recurring act/effect itself, not the people — e.g. “a tongue licking an ice-cream cone”
form.conceptHelp: The trigger word is the token you type to summon this concept. Import raw images of it, then caption and train.
form.styleHelp: Always-on Style: import varied images, then caption every kept image with content only (subject, action, setting) while leaving the aesthetic unspoken. Combine it with a character LoRA by adjusting each LoRA weight.
form.characterHelp: The trigger word is the unique token you will type in prompts to summon this character.

tile.trainedFamily: A {family} LoRA has been trained from this dataset
tile.zipReady: Download the kept images and captions as a training-ready ZIP
tile.zipDisabled: Keep at least one image before exporting a training ZIP
tile.backupTitle: Download a portable backup with all images, captions and settings
tile.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete the dataset “{name}” and all its images? This cannot be undone.

tileSize.small: Compact list — maximum density, browse many datasets at once

captionTools.title: Caption tools
captionTools.intro: Captions are the text the LoRA reads each image by. These tools edit every kept caption at once. Use them to fix a word that slipped into all captions, or to remove or rename a repeating tag. Text mode replaces a phrase anywhere; tag mode matches a whole comma-separated tag (best for booru / SDXL).
captionTools.tagModeHelp: Tag mode treats captions as comma-separated tags: the whole tag must match (case-insensitive), and removal keeps the commas clean. Recommended for booru (SDXL).
captionTools.filterHelp: Exclude hides images already tagged with a word, while Only with isolates the images that have it.
captionTools.proseMatchHelp: Captions here are prose, so a filter matches a whole word (case-insensitive): “smile” matches “a warm smile” but not “smiling”.
captionTools.tagMatchHelp: Captions here are comma-separated tags, so a filter matches one whole tag exactly (case-insensitive).
captionTools.excludeTitle: Hide every image that already carries this tag from the grid
captionTools.onlyWithTitle: Show only images that carry this tag
captionTools.frequencyHelp: Click a {item} to load it into Find ({mode}); leave Replace empty to remove it from every caption. The ⊘ control hides every image whose caption contains it.
captionTools.frequencyItemTitle: “{value}” appears in {count} caption(s) — click to fill Find ({mode})
captionTools.stopHidingTitle: Images tagged “{value}” are hidden — click to show them again
captionTools.hideTitle: Hide images already tagged “{value}” from the grid

categories.character: Repeated details can become baked into the character. Remove unwanted outfits, poses or settings so they remain prompt-controllable; identity words are handled by the identity-leak check.
categories.concept: These are recurring subject and scene terms. The concept itself must stay absent; remove unrelated repeated details that could become tied to the trigger.
categories.style: Repeated subjects and objects can bias a Style LoRA toward specific content. Keep useful content descriptions, balance accidental repetition, and keep aesthetic, medium or artist terms out.

files.title: Caption files on disk
files.writeTitle: Writes <image>.txt beside each kept image in the dataset folder, in the same format as the ZIP export
files.open: Open the dataset folder in the file explorer
files.styleHelp: content-only sidecars · no activation trigger · overwrites existing .txt
files.defaultHelp: kohya-style sidecar captions, trigger included — overwrites existing .txt

imageBulk.hint: Tick images to curate them in bulk —
imageBulk.clearCaptionsTitle: Delete the selected images' captions; use Caption to regenerate them
imageBulk.kleinUnavailable: Klein is not available in this setup
imageBulk.improveTitle: Creates separate 2 MP candidates sequentially. Excluded: {exclusions}
imageBulk.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete the {count} selected image(s), including their files?
imageBulk.improveConfirm: Create a separate 2 MP Klein improvement candidate for {count} image(s)?
imageBulk.originalsUnchanged: Original images stay unchanged until you review the candidates.

reasons.rescuePair: resolve the Klein rescue pair first
reasons.pendingReview: an improvement is already pending review

toast.partial: Klein improvement queued for {succeeded}/{total} image(s) · {failed} failed or refused{refresh}.
toast.queued: Klein improvement queued for {count} image(s) — originals stay intact.

tileSize.small: Small tiles — more per row, quick overview
tileSize.large: Large tiles — see the full composition before you crop, keep or reject

autoTriage.title: Auto-triage

help.1: Marks undecided, face-scored images: keep when face similarity is at or above the threshold, reject below it.
help.2: It never deletes anything or changes your manual ✓/✕ choices.
help.3: Images with no score are skipped; judge those by eye.
help.4: After Apply, move the slider and Re-apply to re-sort everything auto-triaged in this session.

autoTriage.applyTitle: Marks only scored images; manual ✓/✕ choices are never changed

captions.modeTitle: Caption style — Prose (Z-Image) or Booru tags (SDXL booru-native). Defaults automatically from the dataset type.

recaptionConfirm.character: Re-captioning overwrites the {count} existing caption(s). New captions describe the scene without describing the character identity. Continue?
recaptionConfirm.concept: Re-captioning overwrites the {count} existing caption(s). New captions describe the scene while leaving the recurring concept unspoken. Continue?
recaptionConfirm.style: Re-captioning overwrites the {count} existing caption(s). New captions describe image content while leaving the aesthetic or style unspoken. Continue?

recaptionTitle.character: Re-generates every caption without describing identity (face or hair)
recaptionTitle.concept: Re-generates every caption while keeping the recurring concept unspoken
recaptionTitle.style: Re-generates every caption as content-only text without naming the aesthetic

captions.styleStatusTitle: Every Style image needs a content-only caption: describe subject, action and setting, but do not name the aesthetic, medium or artist. No activation trigger is added.
captions.styleMissing: {count} missing · content-only captions required · no trigger
captions.filterActive: A tag filter is active — the filtered grid is in

leak.cleanTitleConcept: No captions name the concept, so it binds to the trigger. Click to see what was checked.
leak.cleanTitleIdentity: No captions describe hair, face or skin, so identity binds to the trigger. Click to see what was checked.
leak.foundTitleConcept: These captions name the concept, preventing it from binding to the trigger. Click to fix them.
leak.foundTitleIdentity: These captions mention hair, face or skin, preventing identity from binding to the trigger. Click to fix them.
leak.noneTitleConcept: The concept-leak scan runs on captions. Caption the kept images first.
leak.noneTitleIdentity: The identity-leak scan runs on captions. Caption the kept images first.
leak.conceptHelp: A concept leak names the recurring concept itself. These words must stay out of captions or the concept binds to text instead of the trigger “{trigger}”. Describe the person and scene, but leave “{concept}” unspoken.
leak.identityHelp: An identity leak describes who the person is, such as hair, eye or skin colour and facial features. These words must stay out of captions or identity binds to text instead of the trigger “{trigger}”.
leak.liveScan: re-scanned live on every caption change
leak.conceptWatched: The words in the concept description “{concept}” and their basic lexical field, including related body parts and positions.
leak.nothingChecked: Nothing checked yet — the scan runs on captions. Caption the kept images first.
leak.allClearConcept: All clear — every caption leaves the concept unspoken, so it binds to the trigger. This is a real result from {count} caption(s).
leak.allClearIdentity: All clear — the captioner describes pose, clothing, setting and framing without identity. This is a real result from {count} caption(s).
leak.fixConcept: {count} captions name the concept — remove those words or re-caption. Edits save when you click away.
leak.fixIdentity: {count} captions leak identity — remove the highlighted words or re-caption. Edits save when you click away.
leak.recaptionAllTitleConcept: Re-generate every leaking caption while keeping the concept unspoken
leak.recaptionAllTitleIdentity: Re-generate every leaking caption without describing identity
leak.recaptionOneTitleConcept: Re-generate this caption while keeping the concept unspoken
leak.recaptionOneTitleIdentity: Re-generate this caption without describing identity
leak.noneLeft: All clear — no leaking captions remain.

scrape.title: Build from scraped images
scrape.targetTitle: A varied set usually trains better than many near-duplicates

dependencies.missing: The optional scraper packages are not installed (curl_cffi, gallery-dl, cloudscraper…). Install them for image previews and imports. Pexels uses its official API for listing, but still needs curl_cffi to fetch images.

reddit.title: Search Reddit
reddit.keywordPlaceholder: keyword — what to look for (e.g. film portrait)
reddit.keywordTitle: Search all of Reddit for a keyword
reddit.communityTitle: Limit the search to one subreddit
reddit.help: Keyword searches all of Reddit for a term. Add a subreddit (the part after r/) to search inside one community. Subreddit alone browses that community's top posts.

pexels.authError: Pexels rejected the request. Check the API key and explicit dataset/ML authorization.
pexels.authorizationBody: An API key alone does not authorize dataset or machine-learning use. Search only if Pexels explicitly authorized your use case.
pexels.confirm: I confirm I have explicit Pexels authorization for dataset/ML use.

url.label: Gallery or media URL
url.placeholder: Gallery, album, collection or direct photo URL
url.help: Use this for supported galleries and albums, or direct Pexels photos and collections. Normal Pexels keyword searches belong in the Pexels tab.
url.fullAlbumsTitle: Off: one listing cover per gallery. On: every photo from each matched gallery.
url.fullAlbums: Scan full albums — off = one cover per gallery, on = every photo of each

rescue.title: Rescue images under 768 px with Klein (generative)
rescue.description: Off by default. Only small images are sent to Klein. The original is preserved, and neither version enters training until you choose one in Curation.
rescue.notReady: Klein is not ready in this setup.

results.deadTitle: Images whose source link is dead or expired — hidden from the grid.
results.previewTitle: Preview size — enlarge to judge images faster
results.filtersReview: Filters at import: duplicates, Klein review for short side < 768px, ratio > 3:1
results.filtersSkip: Filters at import: duplicates, skip short side < 768px, ratio > 3:1

datasetSettings.title: Dataset settings
datasetSettings.name: Name

character.label: 🧑 Character
character.hint: A person or face — identity binds to the trigger

concept.label: 💡 Concept
concept.hint: A recurring act or effect — the concept binds to the trigger

style.label: 🎨 Style
style.hint: An always-on aesthetic: control its influence with the LoRA weight

datasetSettings.styleNotice: no activation trigger is written into captions or prompts. Control the effect with the LoRA weight; when combining with a character LoRA, tune the two weights independently.
datasetSettings.triggerHelp: The word you put in prompts to summon this LoRA. Safe to change anytime — it is added at export, so existing captions do not need redoing.
datasetSettings.conceptDescription: Concept description — what captions must OMIT
datasetSettings.conceptPlaceholder: e.g. a mirror selfie / a specific pose / an art style
datasetSettings.conceptHelp: This is the thing the LoRA learns. Captions describe everything except this, so it binds to the trigger. Editing it rebuilds the auto avoid-list — re-caption to apply it to images already captioned.

suffixes.title: Prompt suffixes
suffixes.placeholder: e.g. shot on 35mm film, warm tones
suffixes.help: Free text added to every generated variation — the identity lock is untouched. A framing suffix applies to that shot type first, then the global one. Applied at generation time: safe to change anytime; existing images stay as they are (regenerate to apply).

switch.title: Changing kind: {from} → {to}

captionStrategy.character: Captions will describe the scene without naming the character's identity (face, hair, skin).
captionStrategy.concept: Captions will describe the scene while leaving the recurring concept unspoken, so it binds to the trigger.
captionStrategy.style: Captions will describe image content only, leaving the aesthetic or style unspoken.

changes.characterPanelsAvailable: The Reference photo, Generate variations and Face analysis panels become available (build the set from a reference).
changes.characterPanelsHidden: The Reference photo, Generate variations and Face analysis panels are hidden — build the set from Import or Scrape instead.
changes.styleTrigger: No activation trigger: a style is always on once loaded — control it with the LoRA weight. Your trigger word is kept but no longer written into captions or prompts.
changes.triggerReturns: The trigger word returns: the stored token is prefilled — set the word you will type in prompts to summon this LoRA.
changes.conceptDescription: A concept description is required — the recurring thing every caption must omit.
changes.fidelityUnavailable: The face / body fidelity setting no longer applies (it is character-only).

kept.images: Every image, its caption text, keep/reject status, face scores and watermark work stay exactly as they are.
kept.runs: Past training runs and checkpoints keep their identity — runs are named by the model family and trigger, never the kind.
kept.conceptDescription: Your concept description is remembered, so switching back restores it.

switch.recaption: Existing captions were written for {kind}. They are not rewritten automatically — use 🔄 Re-caption in the Captions section to apply the new strategy.

sections.forcedOpen: Sections stay open while a search or filter is active

header.styleTitle: This Style LoRA is always active when loaded; adjust its LoRA weight to control the effect.
header.copyTrigger: Copy the trigger word (to put in your prompts)
header.moreTitle: More dataset actions — edit settings, body fidelity
header.editStyleTitle: Edit the Style dataset name and review its always-on behavior.
header.editSettingsTitle: Edit the dataset name, trigger word, and (for concept datasets) the concept description that drives the caption avoid-list.
header.bodyFidelityOnTitle: Body fidelity ON: captions also omit tattoos/scars/marks (they bind to the trigger), composition targets more bust/body shots, imports keep the full frame by default. Click to go back to face-only.
header.bodyFidelityOffTitle: Face-only fidelity (default): the LoRA learns the face; body shape follows the prompt. Click for FULL-BODY fidelity (body shape & marks bind to the trigger too).

confirm.exportWithTriage: {count} image(s) still await triage (✓/✕) and will NOT be in the ZIP. Export anyway?
confirm.exportWithoutCaptions: {count} kept image(s) without a caption (trigger only). Export anyway?
confirm.generationAlreadyRunning:
A generation batch is already running ({count} in flight).

Launch another one anyway?

prompt.datasetFolder: Path of the dataset folder on this machine (images + same-name .txt captions):

toast.autoCropSaveFailed: Could not save the auto-crop preference
toast.styleCaptionsMissing: Style training needs a content-only caption for every kept image — {count} still missing.
toast.bodyFidelityOn: Body fidelity ON — re-caption to apply to existing captions

dataTransfer.importZipTitle: Merge a ZIP of images and kohya-style same-name .txt captions into this dataset. Folder layout is flexible; aspect ratios are kept and perceptual duplicates skipped.
dataTransfer.importFolderTitle: Merge a local folder of images and kohya-style same-name .txt captions into this dataset, including subfolders. Aspect ratios are kept and perceptual duplicates skipped.
dataTransfer.importHelp: merges images + same-name .txt captions in — duplicates are skipped
dataTransfer.backupTitle: Full portable backup: all images, statuses, captions, scores and settings. Restore it on any machine from the Datasets page.
dataTransfer.backupHelp: portable copy — restore it on any machine from the Datasets page
dataTransfer.publishTitle: Publish kept images and captions as a Hugging Face dataset repository. Private by default; choose a license and confirm sharing rights.
dataTransfer.publishHelp: dataset repo on the Hub — private by default

workspace.trainingUnavailable: Training needs ai-toolkit (local GPU) or a vast.ai API key (cloud) — configure either one in Settings.

studioLauncher.title: LoRA testing studio
studioLauncher.configure: Configure ComfyUI in Settings to use the LoRA testing Studio.

activity.comfyPausedDuringPass: ComfyUI is paused during the pass.
activity.caption: Captioning…
activity.gpuProcessing: GPU processing in progress (analysis / cropping / captioning)… ComfyUI is paused during the pass.

curationTools.faceTitle: Scores each image’s facial resemblance to the reference; deletes nothing.
curationTools.findTitle: Scans kept images for overlaid watermarks, logos and URLs; deletes nothing.
curationTools.lamaTitle: LaMa: fast and non-generative. Crops border marks, repaints small off-center marks, and sends on-subject marks to manual review.
curationTools.kleinTitle: Klein: masked FLUX.2 inpainting. Better on skin, fabric and busy backgrounds; can clean marks on the subject. Uses ComfyUI and is slower.
curationTools.kleinUnavailable: Klein inpainting requires ComfyUI running with the Klein models installed (Setup › ComfyUI).
curationTools.autoCropOnTitle: Auto-crop is on: border watermarks are cropped without inventing pixels. Click to repaint them instead. Saved as a preference.
curationTools.autoCropOffTitle: Auto-crop is off: border watermarks are repainted with LaMa/Klein. Click to allow cropping again. Saved as a preference.
curationTools.cleanKleinCrop: Uses masked FLUX.2 Klein inpainting: border marks are cropped; all other marks are repainted and composited back so only the mark changes.
curationTools.cleanKleinNoCrop: Auto-crop is off: every mark, including border marks, is repainted with masked FLUX.2 Klein inpainting and composited back.
curationTools.cleanLamaCrop: Crops border marks, inpaints small off-center marks with LaMa, and flags on-subject marks for manual review.
curationTools.cleanLamaNoCrop: Auto-crop is off: border marks are repainted with LaMa; large or on-subject marks are flagged for manual review.
curationTools.cleanCropOnly: Crops border marks. LaMa inpainting needs a one-time installation; off-center marks are skipped until it is installed.
curationTools.reviewTitle: Step through flagged images, inspect each detected box, then clean, dismiss a false positive or reject.
curationTools.installTitle: Install LaMa watermark inpainting so off-center marks can be repainted instead of only cropped. One-time download of several hundred MB.
curationTools.installDescriptionAfter: package. It downloads CPU PyTorch once (several hundred MB). No restart or GPU is needed; after installation, small off-center marks are inpainted instead of skipped.
curationTools.purgeConfirm: Permanently delete {count} rejected or failed image(s), including their files?
curationTools.purgeTitle: Permanently delete rejected and failed images
curationTools.purgeHelp: frees disk space; rejected images are never used for training

badges.curation: {watermarks} watermark(s) and {rescues} Klein rescue pair(s) to review

scrapeLink.title: Scrape images from the web
scrapeLink.description: scan a gallery URL, pick images, import full-frame

reference.title: Reference photo
reference.intro: one clear photo of the face — every generated variation starts from it
reference.description: source of Klein variations — crop with ✂ after upload
reference.autoCropTitle: ON: a vision pass finds the head and crops around it (slower, pauses ComfyUI). OFF (default): instant centered square — adjust with ✂ Crop, usually faster.
reference.autoCropUnavailableTitle: Auto head-crop is unavailable during local generation; the reference imports with a centered crop.
reference.addExtraLabel: Add an extra reference photo (other angles of the same face)
reference.addExtraTitle: Add an extra reference photo — every engine (Nano Banana, ChatGPT, Klein) uses them together to lock the identity

import.title: Import real photos
import.description: drag and drop or click (normalized to 1024, kept)
import.autoCropTitle: ON: each photo is auto-cropped to a square head shot (vision pass, pauses ComfyUI). OFF: the photo keeps its original framing — use for bust/body shots.
import.autoCropUnavailableTitle: Auto head-crop is unavailable during local generation; photos import full-frame.

composition.title: Composition
composition.missingHint: generate or import these types (target ≈25 balanced)
composition.reached: Target composition reached — ready to caption/export
composition.upscaledHint: these tiles are LANCZOS-enlarged crops (fabricated detail, not native resolution); add native shots for that framing instead of only cropping in

copy.reference: Add the reference photo — one clear face shot is enough to start.
copy.generate: Generate ~25 varied shots from the reference (or import real photos).
copy.curate: Review the grid: keep ✓ the good ones, reject ✕ the rest.
copy.caption: Caption the kept images — required for training.
copy.finish_export: Your dataset is ready — export the ZIP for training.
copy.finish_train: Your dataset is ready — launch a LoRA training.
copy.studio: Compare checkpoints in the Test Studio and pick the best one.

captionEditor.title: Edit caption
captionEditor.truncatedWarning: This caption is exactly 800 characters and ends mid-sentence — an earlier version of the app capped captions there. The cut-off text can’t be recovered; re-caption this image to regenerate the full description.
captionEditor.shortDescription: A brief alternative, trained alongside the long caption. Same rules apply (no trigger, keep the identity/concept/aesthetic out). Leave empty to reuse the long caption; (re-)captioning regenerates it automatically.
captionEditor.shortcuts: Esc to close · Ctrl/⌘ + Enter to save

promptEditor.title: Edit prompt & regenerate
promptEditor.placeholder: describe the shot (the face is kept automatically)…
promptEditor.label: Edit the generation prompt

crop.title: Crop image
crop.selectionLabel: Crop selection — drag to move, use the handles to stretch
crop.freeHint: free = stretch the box any way you like
crop.resetTitle: Re-run the automatic head-crop on the original image

lightbox.cropTitle: Open the crop editor for this image (stretchable box, any ratio)
lightbox.improveHelp: Klein creates a new 2 MP version to validate and leaves the original intact.
lightbox.kleinUnavailable: Klein is not available in this setup.
lightbox.improvementReady: A new version is waiting for validation.
lightbox.improvementPending: An improvement is already running for this image.

generation.stopHint: First results look wrong? Stop now — remaining API calls are skipped and not billed.
generation.stopTitle: Cancel every generation still in flight; finished images stay.

images.empty: No images — generate variations or import photos.
images.noFilterMatches: No images match the active filters —
images.rejectRegenerateConfirm:
Photo rejected — regenerate a new attempt of this variation?
(OK = replace with a new attempt · Cancel = reject only)

variations.title: Generate variations
variations.subtitle: pick the shots to synthesize from the reference photo
variations.loadFailed: Could not load the variation catalog

engine.title: Engine
engine.description: where the images are made — Klein runs free on your GPU · APIs bill per image (or use your ChatGPT subscription)
engine.switchBlocked: A generation batch is running — wait for it to finish before switching engine
engine.kleinDescription: Runs on this machine — slower, with tunable face fidelity.
engine.connectChatGpt: ⚠ Add an API key or connect a subscription in Settings
engine.kleinAssetsMissing: ⚠ Klein {assets} missing — download it in the Setup step
engine.kleinModelMissing: ⚠ Klein model missing — download it in the Setup step (models/unet/klein/)

presets.title: Presets
presets.saveLabel: Save the current shot selection as a custom preset

balanced_25.name: Balanced
balanced_25.hint: The all-round default: every framing covered in training proportions.

zimage_12.name: Z-Image 12
zimage_12.hint: Compact 12-shot set tuned for Z-Image LoRA training.

balanced_multiformat.name: Multi-format
balanced_multiformat.hint: Balanced set with landscape, vertical and cinema frames mixed in.

face_focused.name: Face-focused
face_focused.hint: Face only: close-ups and busts in varied formats, with no body shots.

fullbody_focused.name: Full-body
fullbody_focused.hint: Reliable full-body mix with roughly equal identity and full-body/back coverage in varied formats.

body_emphasis.name: Body emphasis
body_emphasis.hint: Body-fidelity set with figure-revealing, API-safe outfits. Use local Klein for explicit content.

confirm.allDuplicates:
All {count} selected shot(s) already exist in the dataset (green ✓×N cards).

Generate them again anyway?
confirm.someDuplicates:
{duplicates} of {total} selected shots already exist.

OK — generate everything, including duplicates
Cancel — only generate the {fresh} new shot(s)
confirm.cost:
This will launch {count} API generation(s), approximately ${cost} with {engine}.

Proceed?

shotPicker.title: Shots
shotPicker.description: a preset pre-selects a balanced mix — click any card to add or remove it
shotPicker.quotaTitle: Your dataset contains {have} {framing} image(s). Balanced-training target: {target}. This quota does not change the generation selection.
shotPicker.existingCount: {count} image(s) of this shot are already in the dataset

customShots.title: Custom
customShots.kleinOnly: 🔞 shot — switch the generator to Klein

nsfw.title: NSFW mode
nsfw.description: uncensored body shots — generated locally by Klein, never sent to an API
nsfw.captionHint: Captions keep describing the state so it remains promptable and does not bind to the trigger word. The captioner handles this automatically.

customShot.title: Custom shot
customShot.description: write your own prompt — it becomes a reusable card in the Custom group above
customShot.instructions: Describe the outfit, pose and setting, choose a framing, then add it.
customShot.placeholder: e.g. full body shot, sitting on a vintage motorbike in a garage, leather jacket, warm light

suffix.title: Prompt suffixes
suffix.description: creative direction added to every generated shot
suffix.placeholder: e.g. shot on 35mm film, warm tones
suffix.hint: Free text appended to every generated variation. The identity lock is unchanged. A framing suffix applies first, then the global suffix. Saved to the dataset when you generate.

kleinTuning.title: Klein tuning
kleinTuning.hint: Anchors composition, not the face. Higher values suppress pose and framing changes. About 0.5 is balanced; use 0.2–0.4 for major restaging; 0 disables it.
kleinTuning.presetDescription: your own LoRA combinations — applied to every shot in this run
kleinTuning.noPresets: No presets yet — build combinations of your own LoRA files in
kleinTuning.emptyPreset: This preset is empty — add LoRA files to it in Settings.

actions.clearTitle: Clear the whole selection, including presets and shots
actions.multiplierTitle: Generate each selected shot this many times
actions.alreadyRunning: A generation batch is already running

add.title: Add images
add.description: Generate AI variations from the reference — and mix in real photos (import or scrape).
add.conceptDescription: Drop photos, or scrape galleries from the Scrape section — a concept LoRA learns from real images.
add.styleDescription: Import varied images that share the aesthetic; subject and scene diversity keep the Style LoRA composable.

images.title: Images
images.description: Everything in the dataset — keep ✓ the good shots, reject ✕ the rest, edit a caption right on its tile.

scrape.title: Scrape
scrape.description: Scan a gallery URL, pick the images you want, and import them full-frame — crop each one afterwards on its tile.

curation.title: Curation
curation.description: Quality passes over the kept images — face resemblance, watermark find & clean, cleanup.

captions.title: Captions
captions.description: Captions are what training reads each image by — generate them, watch for leaks, edit in bulk.

export.title: Import & export
export.description: Merge an existing dataset in — or get this one out: training ZIP, portable backup, Hugging Face.

training.title: Training
training.description: Turn the kept & captioned images into a LoRA — locally or in the cloud.

checkpoints.title: Checkpoints & LoRAs
checkpoints.description: Review training saves, pick an epoch, import LoRAs into ComfyUI, and clean up old files.

studio.title: Studio
studio.description: Test the trained LoRA with saved winning settings in the dedicated Studio.

scraping.sourceCredentialsHelp: Credentials used when scanning image sources for concept datasets. Reddit and Civitai keys are optional; Pexels requires its API key. Keys are write-only: fields stay blank even when a key is already saved.
scraping.redditHelp: Out of the box, Reddit scans use a public client ID shared by many people. Its ~1000 requests / 10 min quota can be exhausted by others, which appears as “Reddit is rate limiting requests, retry in Ns” (429) even on your first scan of the day. Your own client ID gives you a private quota.

redditGuide.summary: How to create your client ID (takes 1 minute)
redditGuide.step1End: at the bottom of the page.
redditGuide.step2After: ID includes a client secret, so Reddit refuses the secret-free anonymous login used by this app and every scan fails with 401.
redditGuide.step3Before: Name: anything (for example, “LoRA Dataset Studio”). Redirect URI:
redditGuide.step3After: — the form requires one, but it is never used here.
redditGuide.step4After: , then copy the short string shown directly below the app name (about 22 characters). That is your client ID. Installed apps have no secret, so this string is all you need.
redditGuide.step5: Paste it below and save. It takes effect immediately; no restart is needed.

scraping.civitaiHelp: Only needed for adult content. Without a key, Civitai scans return SFW results only. Create one under civitai.com → Account settings → API Keys.
scraping.pexelsHelpBefore: Required for every Pexels scan through the official API.
scraping.pexelsCreateKey: Create a free Pexels API key
scraping.pexelsHelpAfter: — the free quota is 200 requests/hour and 20,000/month. Save it here and it takes effect immediately without a restart.
scraping.pexelsAuthorizationBody: An API key alone does not authorize dataset or machine-learning use. Configure and use this integration only if Pexels has explicitly authorized this use case.
scraping.pexelsTerms: Read the official Pexels terms and conditions
scraping.improvementHelp: Optional instruction for automatically rescuing scraped images under 768 px. Manual Upscale & improve uses its dedicated metadata-derived quality profile. Klein creates a separate 2 MP version for validation and leaves the original intact.
scraping.instructionHelp: Leave this empty to let Klein use the reference image alone. Add a short instruction only when you want to guide automatic scraper rescue; Klein remains generative and may change details. Manual lightbox improvement uses its dedicated quality profile.

localTools.hfTokenHelp: Needed for gated training bases (Krea 2, FLUX.1, FLUX.2 Klein) and for reading your private custom-base cloud repositories. Accept each model license, then create a token at hf.co/settings/tokens. Local Klein generation (9B KV) downloads without a token.
localTools.comfyHelp: Local Klein generation and the Test Studio. The API URL is where a running ComfyUI answers; the install directory is scanned for checkpoints and LoRAs.
localTools.comfyDirHelp: Used to derive the output/input/models/loras folders unless overridden.
localTools.ollamaHelp: Lightweight local vision backend for captioning, framing auto-classification and head-crop.
localTools.ollamaStartFailed: Ollama did not start. Check the log or start it manually.
localTools.aitoolkitHelp: The training engine. Point to the folder containing run.py; its venv/ or .venv/ is detected automatically.
localTools.pythonPlaceholder: Auto — only needed when ai-toolkit has no venv/.venv (conda, uv or system Python)
localTools.pythonHelp: Full path to the Python executable ai-toolkit should use, for example C:\miniconda3\envs\aitk\python.exe.

captioning.captioningHelp: Choose who writes captions. Auto prefers JoyCaption through ai-toolkit and falls back to the Ollama vision model.
captioning.watermarkHelp: Choose where LaMa removes small off-center watermarks. Auto uses CUDA when the configured ML Python supports it and otherwise falls back to CPU.

devices.cuda: GPU (CUDA required; pauses ComfyUI while cleaning)

captioning.allowCropHelp: On: a watermark on a border is cropped off without inventing pixels. Off: border marks are repainted with LaMa/Klein. You can override this for each image in watermark review or from the Clean bar.
captioning.faceHelp: Every image is scored against the reference face with InsightFace. These thresholds control where the badges change.
captioning.thresholdHelp: Green marks a strong match to the reference face. Orange is borderline; review it before keeping. Anything below orange is likely a different person and worth rejecting.

bank.title: Image bank triage
bank.help: Thresholds used to flag low-quality or similar images in the image bank. Flagging remains reversible and never deletes source files.
bank.sharpnessHelp: Images below this Laplacian sharpness score are flagged as blurry.
bank.noiseHelp: Images above this estimated noise level are flagged as noisy.
bank.uniformityHelp: Images above this flat-area ratio are flagged as overly uniform.
bank.minSideHelp: Images whose shortest side is below this pixel size are flagged as small.
bank.duplicateDistanceHelp: Lower values require closer perceptual-hash matches when grouping near-duplicates.
bank.personSimilarityHelp: Minimum face-embedding similarity used to group images of the same person.
bank.aestheticHelp: Scored images below this value are flagged as low aesthetic quality.
bank.nsfwHelp: Scored images at or above this probability are flagged as NSFW.
bank.styleSimilarityHelp: Minimum visual-embedding similarity used to group images by style.

dialogs.presetNamePrompt: Preset name (an existing name is overwritten):
dialogs.resumeConfirm: Resume training “{name}” from step {step} and continue for +1000 steps (→ {next})?
dialogs.moveTrashConfirm:
Move “{filename}” to the trash?

Recoverable until you empty the trash in Settings.
dialogs.cleanupRunConfirm:
Clean up this run?

Keeps {keepCount} checkpoint(s) ({keepList}) and moves {removed} to the trash — recoverable until you empty the trash in Settings.

training.defaultsHelp: Preselected model family for new training runs. Each dataset can still override it.
training.cloudGpuHelp: No local GPU? The app can rent one per run. The key below unlocks the ☁️ Train in cloud button.
training.vastKeyHelp: Enables cloud GPU training. The app rents a GPU for the run and shuts it down when complete (a typical run costs $1–2). Get a key at cloud.vast.ai → Keys.

vastGuide.summary: How to get a vast.ai API key (about 2 minutes)
vastGuide.step2Middle: in the left sidebar and click
vastGuide.step2After: . $5 is enough to start. A typical run costs about $1–2 and is billed by vast.ai, not this app.
vastGuide.step3After: in the left sidebar under Account and copy your API key. Create one first if the list is empty.
vastGuide.step4Before: Paste the key below and press
vastGuide.step4After: . The key is saved automatically and the test should report “connected as <your account>”.

training.cloudHelp: vast.ai GPU rental guardrails: simultaneous training pods, offer price ceiling, monthly spending limit, and the time a run may go without step progress before it is rescued and stopped.
training.minReliabilityHelp: Lower it (for example, 0.95) to show cheaper hosts in the GPU picker, with a higher risk of a pod that never boots. Failed starts usually waste only a few cents and are cleaned automatically.
training.verifiedOnlyHelp: Only show hosts verified by vast.ai. Recommended. Turning this off can reveal more or cheaper offers with greater host risk.
training.secureOnlyHelp: Only show offers marked Secure Cloud by vast.ai. This excludes Community Cloud machines, so availability may be lower and prices higher.

server.title: Server
server.help: Where the app listens. Host, port and LAN access require a restart. Edit them below, then use “Save & restart to apply”.
server.lanAccessHelp: Off by default: only this computer can open the app. On: any device on your Wi-Fi/LAN can open it, including your phone, using the URL below.
server.requireTokenOn: On: remote devices must open the URL with the token once; a session cookie takes over afterwards. This adds safety on a shared or untrusted network.
server.requireTokenOff: Off by default: anyone on your Wi-Fi/LAN can open the app without a password. This is suitable for a home network; enable it for a shared or untrusted network.
server.accessTokenHelp: Remote devices present this once through the link below, then a signed session cookie takes over. Requests from this computer never need it.
server.tokenPlaceholder: (created automatically on the next restart — or click “Generate new token”)
server.phoneHelp: Point your phone camera at the code or open a link below. The LAN link requires the same Wi-Fi; the Tailscale link works from anywhere.
server.tokenNeededAfter: (or save and restart). The scannable link appears after a token exists.
server.addressMissingBefore: Could not detect this machine’s address. From another device, open

maintenance.updatesHelp: Pull the latest version from GitHub and restart without leaving the app.
maintenance.updatedRestarting: ↻ Updated — the app is restarting. This page reloads automatically when it returns…
maintenance.downloadingInstalling: ⬇ Downloading and installing the latest release…
maintenance.logClosedHelp: Something failed? Open this and copy the log into your bug report.
maintenance.openTrashFailed: Could not open the trash folder.
maintenance.emptyConfirm:
Permanently delete everything in the trash?

This is the only destructive action. Deleted checkpoints cannot be recovered afterwards.
maintenance.trashHelp: Everything the app deletes (checkpoints, cloud staging and deployed LoRAs) is moved here first. Emptying it is the only action that actually destroys files.
maintenance.openTrashTitle: Open the trash folder in the file explorer
maintenance.dataHelp: Where dataset images live on disk.

cloudRuns.title: Training runs
cloudRuns.subtitle: Every training in one place — cloud and local: watch progress, stop a run, download a finished LoRA, and see the exact settings each launch used.

thumbnail.open: Last sample this run generated (open full size)

podKept.title: Pod kept for manual checkpoint recovery
podKept.body: it keeps billing until reaped. Download its LoRA, then it is cleaned up automatically after the recovery window.
podKept.collapsedWarning: a kept pod is still billing — expand for details

recipeWarning.title: Z-Image recipe warning:
recipeWarning.blocked: Retry and Continue are disabled; start a fresh validated run.

settings.effectiveTitle: The effective ai-toolkit settings this launch used

confirm.stopCloud:
Stop the cloud run for « {name} »?

The pod is terminated. Any checkpoint reached so far is still downloaded and importable — you only lose the remaining steps.
confirm.stopLocal:
Stop the local run for « {name} »?

The training process is terminated and the pending local training queue is cleared. Checkpoints already saved remain available.
confirm.cleanFinished:
Move the staging folders of all FINISHED runs to the trash?

Dataset copies, samples and checkpoint duplicates already imported. Active runs and pods kept for recovery are spared. Recoverable until you empty the trash in Settings.

prompt.additionalSteps: Additional steps to train from the last checkpoint:

toast.stopCloudFailed: Could not stop the run — it may have already finished.
toast.stoppingCloud: Stopping the run — the pod is winding down…
toast.stopLocalFailed: Could not stop the local run — it may have already finished.
toast.localStopped: Local training stopped — ComfyUI is re-enabled.
toast.stopLocalErrorDetail: Could not stop the local run: {error}
toast.stopLocalError: Could not stop the local run. Please try again.
toast.legacyRetryBlocked: This run uses an incompatible legacy Z-Image recipe. Start a fresh validated run instead.
toast.relaunchedLocal: Run relaunched locally — watch it under In progress…
toast.relaunchedCloud: Run relaunched — provisioning a fresh pod…
toast.legacyContinueBlocked: This checkpoint uses an incompatible legacy Z-Image recipe and cannot be continued safely.
toast.positiveSteps: Enter a positive number of extra steps.
toast.continuing: Continuing from step {from} → {to} on a fresh pod…
toast.configBuildFailed: Could not build the config file — please retry.
toast.configDownloadFailed: Could not download the config file.
toast.cleaned: Cleaned {count} run(s) — {size} GB moved to the trash.

actions.stopLocalTitle: Stop this local training process; checkpoints already saved are kept
actions.retryDisabled: Disabled: this legacy/incompatible Z-Image recipe cannot be replayed safely; start a fresh run
actions.retryLocalTitle: Relaunch this run locally with the same settings
actions.retryCloudTitle: Relaunch this run with the same settings on a fresh pod
actions.continueDisabled: Disabled: this legacy/incompatible Z-Image checkpoint cannot be continued safely; start a fresh run
actions.continueTitle: Resume training from this run's last checkpoint for more steps, on a fresh pod
actions.shareConfigTitle: Download this run's full settings as a paste-safe text file (recipe / help thread)
actions.vastInstanceTitle: vast.ai instance {id} — provider console (billing, logs, manual destroy)
actions.vastConsoleTitle: vast.ai console — billing, logs, manual destroy
actions.showDatasetRuns: Show the runs of this dataset
actions.foldDatasetRuns: Fold the runs of this dataset

notConfigured.before: Cloud training isn’t configured yet. Add your vast.ai API key in

empty.inProgress: No run in progress. Launch one from a dataset’s training panel.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.description: Install the app, connect the tools you need, and understand the workspace.

usingTheApp.title: Using the app
usingTheApp.description: Follow the complete workflow for character, concept, and style datasets.

datasetGuide.title: Building a good dataset
datasetGuide.description: Make stronger choices about images, captions, settings, and checkpoints.

settingsReference.title: Settings reference
settingsReference.description: Every setting explained — what it does, its default, and when to change it.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Find a symptom, understand the cause, and apply the shortest reliable fix.

gettingHelp.title: Getting help
gettingHelp.description: Create a useful report and share the details needed to solve a problem.

diagnostic.title: One-click bug report
diagnostic.description: Copies version, environment health (Python/Pillow/disk), per-engine status with the exact missing Klein assets, live ComfyUI GPU/VRAM/queue, the last generation failures and the last error tracebacks — no API keys, no folder paths (your home dir is redacted to ~). The log/error lines can still mention file names: skim before posting.
diagnostic.copied: Diagnostic report copied — paste it into your bug report.

studio.title: Test Studio
studio.requiresComfyUI: Test Studio requires ComfyUI — configure it in Settings.
studio.emptySelection: Check a LoRA above to tune and test it. Check ≥2 to compare them side by side.

loraPicker.loadFailed: Could not load the LoRA list.
loraPicker.title: LoRA to test
loraPicker.empty: No trained LoRA is available. Train one from a dataset first.
loraPicker.familyLocked: A test can only use one model family. Clear the selection to switch families.

solo.noCheckpoint: No checkpoint is available for this LoRA.

strength.title: LoRA strength
strength.showNegativeTitle: Show negative strengths for contrast tests
strength.negativeHint: Negative strengths invert the LoRA effect and are mainly useful for contrast testing.

seed.autoTitle: Use a new seed after each launch
seed.countTitle: Number of images generated per configuration
seed.batchTitle: Each configuration is generated {count} times because batch LoRAs are enabled

run.stopped: {count} stopped cell(s) — resumable with their settings

comparison.empty: Set up the run on the left, then launch the test to compare {count} LoRAs side by side.

results.title: Results
results.voteTitle: Quickly vote on all unrated images (swipe or 👍/👎)
results.revoteTitle: Second pass: re-vote only the 👍 results to narrow them down
results.exportTitle: Compose this run into one shareable image (checkpoints × strengths)
results.exportUnavailable: No finished image to export yet
results.chooseRun: Choose the test run to display

vote.title: Quick vote
vote.hint: ← swipe/left arrow = 👎 · right = 👍 → · Esc = close · use Skip to pass

setup.replaceDescription: Replace the current prompt with the described one?
setup.describeTitle: Describe an image as a test prompt with the vision model
setup.promptPlaceholder: Leave empty for the LoRA's default prompt…
setup.gpuCost: GPU cost: checked LoRAs × strengths × images per configuration{batch}
setup.batchCost: × {count} (batch axis: without and with each checked LoRA)
setup.selectOne: Check at least one LoRA above.

preflight.mismatch: “{name}” is a {detected} LoRA, but this is the {family} Studio. ComfyUI would silently ignore it and render every tile without the LoRA. Test it in the {detected} Studio or deploy it under that family.
preflight.missing: The {family} test pipeline can’t run because ComfyUI is missing the assets below. Add them, then relaunch the test.
preflight.nodes: Missing custom node(s) ({count}) — install into ComfyUI

recent.title: Recent prompts (click to reload · 🗑 to delete) — thumbnail = liked image
recent.deleteConfirm: Delete this recent prompt and its {count} saved test image(s)?
recent.deleteTitle: Delete this recent prompt and its test images

describe.invalidType: Pick a webp, png or jpg image.
describe.empty: The vision model returned an empty description.
describe.failed: Describe failed — check that the app can reach Ollama, then try again.
describe.dialogLabel: Describe an image as a test prompt
describe.title: Describe an image
describe.description: The vision model turns the image into a test prompt covering scene, pose, framing and outfit. It does not name the person or add the trigger word; the Studio handles those.
describe.loadingHint: The vision model may be loading. This can take a few seconds.
describe.drop: Drop an image here, or click to choose
describe.formats: webp, png or jpg · up to {size} MB

generation.outputSizeWithRatio: Output size — the ratio above sets the proportions. Standard ≈ 1 MP.
generation.outputSizeWithAxis: Output size (the aspect axis sets the proportions). Standard ≈ 1 MP.
generation.detailHint: 0.21 = SDXL default · ≤0.25 safe · ↑ more detail (HDR/grain risk)
generation.rebalanceLabel: Krea conditioning rebalance for uncensored output and skin texture
generation.rebalanceHint: Lifts safety-filtered conditioning taps for uncensored output and less plastic skin. Off = pure SFW.
generation.enhancerHint: Model-side text-adherence patch independent of rebalance. Off = workflow unchanged.
generation.precisionHint: Use bf16 if the Enhancer produces black images from fp8 overflow. It is slower but uses the same VRAM.
generation.alwaysOn: Always-on LoRAs (every cell · ⚖ batch = tested as an axis)
generation.noAlwaysOn: No always-on LoRA for this pipeline.
generation.negativePlaceholder: Leave empty for the pipeline default…

best.lowSampleTitle: Recommendation based on few votes — keep voting to make it more reliable
best.saveTitle: Save this configuration as the dataset's best setting

tile.failedTitle: Generation failed — see the Server log in Settings for details.
tile.faceSimilarity: Face similarity to the dataset reference: {score}

grid.caption: Test grid {key}: rows are checkpoints and columns are strengths

export.title: Export grid
export.description: Compose this run into one labelled image (checkpoints × strengths), ready to share.
export.promptHint: Off by default because prompts may be personal or NSFW.
export.downscaleWarning: Large grid — the image will be downscaled to fit the {size}px limit.
export.exportedDownscaled: Grid exported — downscaled to fit the size limit

errorBoundary.inline: Something went wrong. Please refresh the page.

settings.removeConfirm: Remove the saved {label}? Any engine that uses it stops working until you add a new key.

overview.setupBefore: Add at least one image API key to start, or let the
overview.setupAfter: scan your machine and walk you through it.
overview.imageEnginesHint: API keys, ChatGPT subscription, default engine
overview.setupWizardHint: Guided scan + install of everything above

engines.apiKeysHelp: Keys are write-only — fields stay blank even when a key is already saved.
engines.openaiHelp: Powers the ChatGPT (gpt-image-2) engine. Optional if you connect a ChatGPT subscription below.
engines.enginesHelp: Choose which engines appear in the generation panel and which one is selected by default.

subscription.title: ChatGPT subscription (experimental)
subscription.help: Use your ChatGPT Plus/Pro image quota instead of a pay-per-use API key. This unofficial integration may stop working if OpenAI closes it. Compared with API mode: up to 5 reference images, your plan's daily image cap, and SFW only.
subscription.waiting: Waiting for you to enter the code…
subscription.openUrl: 1. Open this address on any device and sign in:
subscription.enterCode: 2. Enter this one-time code (expires in 15 minutes):

authOptions.auto: Auto — use subscription when connected, otherwise API key

subscription.quotaHelp: If the subscription quota runs out during a batch, remaining rows fail with a clear message. The app never silently switches to your paid API key.

presets.title: Klein generation LoRA presets (optional)
presets.help: Create named chains of your own LoRA files after the consistency LoRA in the local Klein engine. Order controls chain order; each preset supports up to {loras} LoRAs and there can be {presets} presets. Files found in ComfyUI models/loras are suggested, with Klein-compatible files first; paths can also be entered manually. Select a preset per run in Klein tuning.
presets.none: No presets yet — create your first combination below.
presets.empty: Empty preset — add a LoRA below.

setup.skip: Skip setup — I'll do it later

welcome.title: Welcome to LoRA Dataset Studio
welcome.description: Let's set up your machine. I'll scan what's already installed and help you install the rest — you can also start building a dataset from your own photos right now, no setup required.
welcome.readySummary: {ready} of {total} capabilities ready. Reachable services were filled in automatically.

scan.trainingPartial: found on disk — one click to use

setup.unsavedBackConfirm:
You have unsaved changes on this step — they will be lost.

Go back without saving?

ollama.title: Ollama — captioning & auto-framing
ollama.modelMissing: Ollama is running, but the vision model has not been pulled yet — it powers captioning.
ollama.modelHint: Use the ABLITERATED Qwen3-VL ({vram}) — the vanilla model refuses NSFW. For the best captions, the app pairs it with JoyCaption (ai-toolkit).
ollama.readyDone: is ready. Nothing to do here.
ollama.installedAt: Ollama is installed at {path}, but is not running.
ollama.installed: Ollama is installed, but is not running.
ollama.startHint: Start it (it listens on port 11434) to unlock captioning and auto-framing — no restart needed.
ollama.gateNotInstalled: Ollama is not installed — download it and start it (port 11434) to continue.
ollama.gateNotRunning: Ollama is installed but not running — click ▶ Start Ollama below to continue.
ollama.gatePullModel: Pull the vision model below to continue — Z-Image captioning needs it (JoyCaption only covers SDXL).
ollama.intro: Ollama runs local models for captioning and auto-framing. Installing it is not enough — you also need to pull a vision model.
ollama.installStep: Install Ollama and start it (defaults to port 11434).

quality.title: Quality tools (ML extras)
quality.unlocks: Face-similarity scoring · Person masks · Watermark inpainting

training.title: LoRA training — ai-toolkit
training.noGpu: No GPU? Skip this step and add a vast.ai API key in Settings to train in the cloud. The app rents one GPU per run (typically $1–2) and shuts it down automatically.
training.readyAt: ✓ ai-toolkit is set up at {path}. Nothing to do here.
training.foundAt: Found an ai-toolkit install at {path}. Use it?
training.notUsable: The configured folder {path} is not usable yet — set up its Python virtual environment as described in the README.
training.intro: ai-toolkit trains the LoRA. Install it once, then point the app at its folder.
training.clone: Clone ai-toolkit and set up its virtual environment as described in its README.

whatsNew.title: What's new
whatsNew.empty: Nothing new yet — check back after the next update.

tips.dataset-settings-modal: Prompt suffixes add creative direction to every generated variation, globally or by framing.
tips.workspace-scrape: Scraping now lives in its own Scrape section in the sidebar.
tips.page-datasets: Resize tiles S/M/L, collapse sections, and filter by kind.

klein.generation_lora_presets: Build named generation-LoRA presets in Settings → Image engines, then pick one per run.

training.dual_captions: Dual captions train each image with both a detailed caption and a shorter caption during local training.

tips.action-watermark-clean: Clean has two engines — LaMa (fast) and Klein (quality) — and auto-crop can be turned off.
tips.continue-training: Continue a run with more steps, resume from an earlier checkpoint, or adjust safe cadence and preview settings.
tips.action-recaption-targeted: You can re-caption just one leaking image (or all of them) — no full re-run.
tips.action-watermark-restore: Not happy with a clean? Restore brings the original back — then try the other engine.
